rulings.Case Title: JOHN GIDDING et al VS. IVAN KAYE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause In Re Contempt (John Giddings)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, April 11, 2014, Line 1, Order To Show Cause In Re Contempt (John Giddings). Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Pursuant to the Order to Show Cause issued December 6, 2013, Plaintiff JOHN GIDDING is ordered to personally appear and show cause why he should not be held in contempt. Plaintiff¿s failure to personally appear will result in a finding of contempt, and a body attachment will issue, with bail set at $25,000. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARY ROSE WARD VS. JULIA DUKHOVNEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Relief From Default Judgment; Motion To Quash; Motion And Notice Of Motion Vacating And Setting Aside Default And Default Judgment And Granting Leave To Defend And Memo Of P&A;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Friday, April 11, 2014, Line 7, DEFENDANT JULIA DUKHOVNY's Motion For Relief From Default Judgment; Motion To Quash; Motion And Notice Of Motion Vacating And Setting Aside Default And Default Judgment And Granting Leave To Defend. Denied without prejudice. Defendant submits the Declaration of [Defendant] Julia Dukhovny as the only evidence in support of her assertion that Defendant was never properly served with the summons and complaint. In opposition, Plaintiff provides deposition testimony from Defendant's husband stating that Defendant's "English is not even good for Sesame Street." (Ex. 3, Deposition of Toly Dukhovny, p. 29:15.) In reply, Defendant does not address Plaintiff's assertion that Defendant does not speak English. Defendant also does not present any evidence showing that she wrote and understood her declaration. Because an oath from a translator was not filed pursuant to Evid. Code sec. 751(c), the motion is denied without prejudice for lack of credible evidence. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JANEVA CHIANG et al VS. NADER HEYDAYIAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Form Interrogatories, Set One; And Special Interrogatories, Set One, And Request For Sanctions As To All Plaintiffs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, April 11, 2014, Line 2, DEFENDANT NADER HEYDAYIAN'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Form Interrogatories, Set One; And Special Interrogatories, Set One, And Request For Sanctions As To All Plaintiffs. Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant¿s Motion to Compel Discovery Responses is GRANTED. The remaining plaintiffs shall serve verified responses, without objection, to Defendant¿s Requests for Production of Documents, Form Interrogatories, Set One, and Special Interrogatories, Set One on or before May 9, 2014. Defendant¿s Request for sanctions is GRANTED as to all remaining plaintiffs in the amount of $1,340.00. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NETSANET HAILEMICHAEL VS. BANK OF THE WEST, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Netsanet Hailemichael'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Deposition Of Defendants Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents, And For An Award Of A Monetary Sanction Against Defendants And Their Counsel In The Amount Of $2716.25
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Friday, April 11, 2014, Line 13, Plaintiff Netsanet Hailemichael'S Motion To Compel Further Deposition Of Defendants Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents, And For An Award Of A Monetary Sanction Against Defendants And Their Counsel In The Amount Of $2716.25. Granted, in part and denied in part. Within 10 days of the hearing date of April 11, 2014, Defendant Boyer shall appear for further deposition unless all parties mutually agree to a later date or Defendant Boyer's answer will be stricken . The parties are instructed to meet and confer regarding the deposition venue and date. The deposition shall occur on consecutive days until completed, and shall not exceed seven hours of deposition. The motion is denied as to further responses to Special Interrogatories and Request for Production of Document. On October 29, 2013, the Court issued an order in which the parties agreed that Defendants had produced all documents necessary and that all issues that Plaintiff had with Defendants' document production had been resolved. Monetary sanctions are denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: PRAVIN PATEL et al VS. SHARAD SHANTILAL PATEL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Friday, April 11, 2014, Line 15, DEFENDANTs VINAY PATEL and RITA PATEL's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Denied. (CCP § 425.16.) Defendants Vinay Patel and Rita Patel (collectively "Defendants') have not met their initial burden of proof to show that they engaged in protected activity. "Filing a false criminal complaint is an illegal activity, not a constitutionally protected exercise of the rights of petition or free speech, and that is the end of [the defendant's] anti-SLAPP motion." (Lefebvre v. Lefebvre (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 696, 706.) Defendants do not proffer any evidence to contest the allegation that they submitted an illegal, false police report. "[W]hen a defendant does not show that a "protected activity" underpins plaintiff's claims, it is irrelevant whether the plaintiff has shown a "probability of prevailing" on his or her claims." (Gerbosi v. Gaims, Weil, West & Epstein, LLP (2011) 193 Cal.App.4th 435, 445.) Accordingly, this motion is frivolous pursuant to CCP § 425.16(c). Plaintiffs are entitled to monetary sanctions against Defendants. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: PRAVIN PATEL et al VS. SHARAD SHANTILAL PATEL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Friday, April 11, 2014, Line 15, DEFENDANTs SHARAD PATEL, JAYSHREE PATEL, DILIP PATEL, SHARMILA PATEL, HARISH PATEL and NALINI PATEL's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. For the first through fifth causes of action, Defendants' demurrer for failure to plead facts is overruled. (CCP § 430.10(e).) Although Plaintiffs failed to attach the 1985 written statement, Plaintiffs have alleged its contents. (First Am. Complaint, 25(A) - 25(E), 25(G), 25(H), 29, 42, 80.) Further, Defendants' statute of frauds argument does not dispose of any entire causes of action, because of allegations pertaining to the 1985 written statement. Plaintiffs have standing to allege the first through fifth causes of action. For the sixth cause of action, Plaintiffs have sufficiently pled the defamatory statements at issue. (Id. at 50, 107.) The claims are not barred by the statute of limitations. Plaintiffs allege that damages were incurred in January 2013. (Id. at 50.) For the seventh cause of action, whether the abuse of process claims are barred by the litigation privilege (CCP § 47(b)) involves a question of fact that is not the proper subject of the demurrer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
